title: hieracium vogesiacum subsp. lagopinum
intro: Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. lagopinum, also known as Hieracium lagopinum, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat kidney stones. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, Hieracium vogesiacum subsp. lagopinum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
